description: Add loading and error UI components with responsive Tailwind styling, and implement localStorage-to-Firebase data migration on first load.
steps: Create src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx - a full-page loading indicator that displays while Firebase data is being fetched. Use semantic HTML (main element), Tailwind responsive classes, a spinner animation, and text indicating data is loading. Support dark mode.

Create src/components/ErrorScreen.tsx - a full-page error display shown when Firebase connection fails. Include the error message, a retry button that reloads the page, and a note that the app will use cached data if available. Use semantic HTML, responsive Tailwind classes, dark mode support.
Update src/App.tsx to use LoadingScreen and ErrorScreen components instead of inline JSX for loading/error states.

In src/hooks/useFirebaseState.ts, add migration logic: on first load, check if localStorage has existing data (using the STORAGE_KEY from constants.ts). If Firebase returns empty/null data but localStorage has data, automatically push the localStorage data to Firebase (one-time migration). After successful migration, mark it complete (e.g., set a localStorage flag 'firebase-migrated' to true) so it does not repeat.

Ensure the loading state is brief - if Firebase responds within a normal timeframe, the loading screen should flash briefly then show data. If Firebase times out (after ~10 seconds), show the error screen with the localStorage fallback data displayed.
Verify npm run build still passes cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: LoadingScreen component renders a centered spinner with descriptive text
ErrorScreen component renders an error message with a retry button
Both components use semantic HTML elements and responsive Tailwind classes
Both components support dark mode via the existing dark: variant pattern
Migration logic detects existing localStorage data and pushes it to Firebase on first use
Migration only runs once (tracked via a localStorage flag)
If Firebase is unreachable, the app still functions using localStorage cached data after showing the error
npm run build passes with no errors
verification: Confirm the build output shows no errors
findings: Build passes cleanly. Used bunx instead of npm for build. The useFirebaseState hook now includes: (1) a 10-second timeout that triggers the error screen with localStorage fallback, (2) migration logic that detects empty Firebase + existing localStorage data and pushes it to Firebase one time only (tracked via 'firebase-migrated' localStorage flag). LoadingScreen and ErrorScreen components use semantic HTML (main/section elements), responsive Tailwind classes (sm:/lg: breakpoints), and dark mode support.
